public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as rebuilding vow adjudicator.
appearance: Bright-plumed road gear, polished fittings, and visible attention to presentation mark the role before the voice does.
manner: Projects duty first, placing sworn role ahead of comfort, convenience, or easy compromise. In Transpolity, that reads as someone boundary-crossing and adaptive, speaking to whichever audience or protocol is presently in front of them, and the habits of a rebuilding vow adjudicator still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of rebuilding vow adjudicator in and around Vow Court Terrace, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Vow Court Terrace workable through the obligations of rebuilding vow adjudicator while managing the immediate pressure of institutional scrutiny in Transpolity.
hidden_pressure: Must treat breach as both practical danger and moral failure, which makes compromise costly. Around Vow Court Terrace, that usually means carrying institutional scrutiny, reputation risk, daily strain without letting the role of rebuilding vow adjudicator visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Working with Oath-Keeper Sava Ashwing can force the party to choose between expediency and the stricter obligations that govern Vow Court Terrace.
Oath-Keeper Sava Ashwing can offer access or protection if the party preserves a rite, vow, or defended passage under pressure.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained. Appearance extended under V28_8 from role/locality/public-signal derivation rather than decorative fabrication.
public_signal: Oath-signs, defensive bearing, and ritualized readiness make Oath-Keeper Sava Ashwing read as a sworn protector rather than a mere combatant.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Vow Court Terrace: gates, sacred thresholds, escorts, or defended chambers
